outlook.headline: ASIC demand broadens beyond design into buildout and test
outlook.summary: Custom silicon demand looks to be improving over the next 1-2 quarters, with hyperscaler AI programs driving more design starts, launches, and follow-on demand across EDA, foundry, IP, and test. The strongest reads are at Cadence, Marvell, Qualcomm, Intel, Synaptics, and Ambarella, while Broadcom also sees solid TPU/ASIC demand; the main offset is that some of the value chain is seeing margin pressure and ramp timing risk rather than demand weakness.

drivers.label: Hyperscaler AI custom chip investment is accelerating design activity
drivers.detail: More design starts, marquee customer engagement, and performance/time-to-market requirements are lifting ASIC demand across the ecosystem.

drivers.label: Broadening demand into major custom-silicon vendors
drivers.detail: Strong pipeline growth, initial shipments, and named hyperscaler ramps point to continued customer pull for custom silicon programs.

drivers.label: Downstream manufacturing/test activity is ramping
drivers.detail: Healthy ASIC/compute ramps are showing up in test and subsystem-related reads, indicating the demand wave is moving beyond tape-out.

drivers.label: Custom silicon remains a growth vector for foundry capacity
drivers.detail: Custom silicon solutions are expected to drive incremental growth over time, reinforcing the buildout phase of the cycle.

drivers.label: Some product-level softness is tied to system preference and margins
drivers.detail: NVIDIA noted customers often want full systems rather than semi-custom chips, and Broadcom flagged lower margins/margin pressure even with solid ASIC demand.

risks: Hyperscaler programs may favor full-system deals over semi-custom ASICs, limiting addressable chip demand in some cases.
First-silicon issues and ramp timing could delay revenue recognition for test and launch-dependent suppliers like Teradyne.
Margin pressure at high-volume ASIC suppliers could temper the perceived quality of the demand rebound.
